summary: Defines config-backed helpers for resolving raw, curated, and output roots so the rest of the codebase never hardcodes data paths.
responsibilities: Resolve storage roots from StorageConfig.
Provide canonical helper accessors for raw, curated, and output directories.
Centralize path layout assumptions used across ingest and build code.

ai.notes: High fan in utility. Even small behavior changes can break many pipelines and tests because most ingestion and build modules route through these helpers.

summary: Generates canonical filenames and paths for versioned datasets, crosswalks, and panels using the project temporal shorthand conventions.
responsibilities: Encode naming rules for boundaries, tracts, counties, measures, panels, and crosswalks.
Normalize ACS vintages and geography scoping in artifact names.
Prevent ad hoc filenames from diverging across producers and consumers.

ai.notes: Acts as a data contract layer for deterministic artifact discovery. Changes can invalidate downstream lookups or tests even when algorithms stay the same.

summary: Registers the top-level Typer application, shared automation flags, and subcommands for ingestion, validation, migration, build, and diagnostics workflows.
responsibilities: Compose the CLI tree and attach subcommand modules.
Enforce non interactive and project root preflight behavior.
Expose agent facing guidance via coclab agents.

ai.notes: High fan out entrypoint. Changes here affect command discovery and automation defaults but most business logic still lives in subcommand modules.

summary: Persists a provenance rich registry of external ingests, including hashes, timestamps, retained raw locations, and source metadata.
responsibilities: Record every upstream source acquisition with history preserved.
Detect upstream changes by comparing source URL, source type, and content hash.
Persist the registry with provenance metadata for reproducibility and audits.

ai.notes: Sits on the ingest boundary. It does not fetch data itself but is the system of record for external artifact lineage and change detection.

summary: Resolves declarative recipes into deterministic materialize, resample, and join tasks with explicit dataset year coverage and geometry choices.
responsibilities: Resolve dataset paths and effective geometries for each requested year.
Select transforms and expand year specs into executable tasks.
Fail fast on missing coverage, ambiguous file set segments, or unresolved template variables.

ai.notes: Pure planning layer with strong validation semantics. Most failures here are contract or configuration errors rather than runtime IO issues.

summary: Executes recipe plans end to end, materializing transforms, resampling datasets, joining yearly outputs, and writing final artifacts with manifests and provenance.
responsibilities: Orchestrate execution state, caching, and step reporting for recipe pipelines.
Resolve transform artifacts and perform resample and join work across geometry types.
Persist outputs, manifests, and diagnostics while tracking consumed assets.

ai.notes: Large orchestration module with many helper paths and Connecticut specific alignment logic. It is likely a change hotspot and a good candidate for future decomposition.

summary: Panel assembly engine for CoC and metro panels. Metro panels support three optional data sources: ACS5 (tract-level, apportioned), ACS1 (metro-level unemployment_rate_acs1 via _load_acs1_metro_measures), and BLS LAUS (metro-level labor_force/employed/unemployed/unemployment_rate via _load_laus_metro_measures when include_laus=True). METRO_PANEL_COLUMNS includes LAUS columns. build_panel accepts include_laus and laus_dir parameters; LAUS integration warns when artifact is missing rather than failing silently.
responsibilities: Load and align PIT, ACS, ACS1, and ZORI inputs across years.
Enforce canonical panel schemas and provenance columns for CoC and metro builds.
Apply alignment and boundary change logic before final persistence.

ai.notes: Central panel composition module with balanced fan in and fan out. Regressions here are highly user visible because it bridges multiple upstream products.

summary: Runs post build validation checks on assembled panels and packages the findings into human readable and JSON safe reports.
responsibilities: Define canonical schema and completeness checks for panel outputs.
Register and execute conformance checks against a PanelRequest.
Convert findings into structured results for CLI and automation consumers.

ai.notes: Quality gate for assembled panels. The dependency on panel.assemble for schema constants is convenient but it also couples validation contracts to producer implementation.

summary: Fetches ACS 5 year tract data from the Census API, derives core demographic measures, snapshots raw responses, and writes curated tract level outputs with provenance.
responsibilities: Validate ACS vintages and normalize tract GEOIDs.
Download per state tract tables and derive adult and poverty metrics.
Register upstream source metadata and persist curated parquet plus raw API snapshots.

ai.notes: Networked ingest path with per state fan out and derived measure logic. Failures here can come from upstream API changes as often as from local code bugs.

summary: Normalizes raw HUD PIT files from CSV and Excel into the canonical CoC by year schema, including CoC ID cleanup and parse diagnostics.
responsibilities: Map source column names into canonical PIT fields.
Normalize and validate CoC identifiers including suffix cleanup.
Emit parsed PIT tables and parse metadata in the canonical schema.

ai.notes: Boundary between messy vendor spreadsheets and the canonical PIT schema. Parsing rules are format sensitive and can break when HUD changes headers or workbook structure.

summary: Embeds and retrieves CoC Lab dataset lineage inside Parquet metadata so artifacts carry their own provenance without sidecar files.
responsibilities: Define the extensible ProvenanceBlock contract used across curated outputs.
Write parquet files with embedded provenance metadata and read it back without loading full datasets.
Generate compact temporal notation from boundary and source vintage fields for downstream lineage inspection.

ai.notes: High fan-in support module used by ingest, panel, and recipe code. Contract changes here can silently affect lineage guarantees across many output writers.

summary: Authoritative Glynn/Fox metro definition data: 25 metros (GF01-GF25) with METRO_DEFINITIONS (id, name, membership_type), METRO_COC_MEMBERSHIP, METRO_COUNTY_MEMBERSHIP, METRO_CBSA_MAPPING (metro_id → 5-digit CBSA), and METRO_STATE_FIPS (metro_id → 2-digit state FIPS for BLS LAUS series ID construction). Lookup helpers: cbsa_to_metro_id, metro_name_for_id, metro_state_fips_for_id. DataFrame builders for each table.
responsibilities: Define the versioned truth tables for metro IDs, membership types, CoCs, counties, and CBSA codes.
Expose dataframe builders and lookup helpers for downstream metro pipelines and tests.
Keep metro naming and source references consistent across PIT, ACS, PEP, and ZORI integrations.

ai.notes: Static reference data with substantial downstream fan-in. Small edits can reassign metro membership across multiple products without any algorithmic change nearby.

summary: Persists raw file and API snapshots under canonical retention paths and computes stable hashes for reproducibility and source change tracking.
responsibilities: Build canonical raw directory and file paths for retained upstream artifacts.
Persist downloaded files with deterministic hashing, including content-stable ZIP hashing.
Write API request, response, and manifest snapshots so fetches can be audited and reproduced later.

ai.notes: Shared ingest utility that sits underneath source retention and hash-based change detection. Behavior changes can invalidate reproducibility assumptions across multiple fetchers.

summary: Defines the Pydantic recipe contract for years, geometries, datasets, transforms, pipelines, and validation policies used by the recipe toolchain.
responsibilities: Enforce structural validation for recipe YAML before semantic planning begins.
Model time-banded file sets, transform specs, pipeline steps, and validation policies.
Provide shared value objects consumed by the loader, planner, adapters, and recipe CLI.

ai.notes: Core contract module for recipe authoring. Schema churn here ripples into validation, planning, CLI output, and tests, so backward-compatibility decisions matter.

summary: Provides the runtime registries and semantic validation pass that connect recipe schema objects to concrete geometry and dataset adapters.
responsibilities: Register geometry and dataset validation adapters keyed by type or provider/product pair.
Report unknown adapter diagnostics when recipe references cannot be resolved semantically.
Aggregate semantic findings across recipe targets, transforms, and datasets after structural parsing.

ai.notes: Small but central glue layer between structural schema validation and adapter-specific semantics. Most risk is contract drift between registry keys and default adapter implementations.

summary: Exposes the recipe CLI commands for validate, plan, preflight, execute, provenance inspection, and export with both human and JSON outputs.
responsibilities: Register default adapters before recipe operations and coordinate loader, planner, executor, and preflight calls.
Translate recipe subsystem exceptions into CLI-safe human and JSON responses.
Format preflight, plan, manifest, and provenance data for automation as well as interactive use.

ai.notes: High fan-out command surface over the recipe subsystem. Changes here affect automation ergonomics and error handling more than the core build algorithms themselves.

summary: Downloads Zillow ZORI county or ZIP feeds, normalizes the wide monthly CSVs into canonical long-form records, and writes curated parquet with provenance.
responsibilities: Manage raw download caching, daily filenames, and source registry updates for Zillow feeds.
Reshape Zillow CSV extracts into validated monthly county or ZIP series with normalized geo IDs.
Persist canonical ZORI outputs with attribution, provenance, and continuity checks for downstream aggregation.

ai.notes: Networked ingest boundary that depends on Zillow file naming and column conventions. Upstream format drift is the primary operational risk, followed by geo ID normalization mistakes.

summary: Fetches county-level ACS totals and materializes weighting tables used to aggregate ZORI from county geography into CoC outputs.
responsibilities: Validate ACS vintage strings and supported weighting methods.
Query Census ACS county totals and snapshot raw responses for reproducibility.
Write canonical county weight artifacts with provenance for downstream ZORI aggregation.

ai.notes: External-API ingest plus contract builder for downstream aggregation. Variable mapping or vintage parsing mistakes would skew every weighted rent series that depends on it.

summary: Combines ZORI inputs, county-to-CoC crosswalks, and ACS-derived weights to build monthly and yearly CoC rent series with coverage diagnostics and provenance.
responsibilities: Load ZORI, crosswalk, and county weight inputs from their canonical artifact locations.
Translate Connecticut legacy county versus planning region mismatches before aggregation when needed.
Compute coverage-aware monthly and yearly CoC ZORI outputs and persist them with provenance metadata.

ai.notes: Multi-input aggregation hotspot with Connecticut-specific alignment logic and threshold-based nulling. Regressions here are user-visible because they directly change published CoC rent outputs.

summary: Aggregates tract-level ACS measures into CoC or other target geographies using crosswalk weights, coverage diagnostics, and limited MOE propagation.
responsibilities: Remap Connecticut planning-region GEOIDs when ACS and crosswalk vintages diverge.
Validate tract overlap before aggregation so tract-vintage mismatches surface early.
Compute count and median outputs for arbitrary target geographies and the CoC convenience path.

ai.notes: Core analytic transformation with methodological caveats documented in-module. Changes can shift demographic estimates across many downstream panels, so validation and documentation have to stay aligned.

summary: Centralizes upstream URLs and filename templates so ingest modules share one authoritative catalog of external data endpoints.
responsibilities: Define stable Census, HUD, and Zillow endpoint constants used across ingestion code.
Prevent hardcoded source URLs from drifting across modules.
Provide a single place to update provider URL templates when upstream hosts or paths change.

ai.notes: Small file with high fan-in. Most risk comes from silent breakage if a provider URL or template changes and downstream ingesters keep assuming the old endpoint.

summary: Discovers, downloads, and records HUD User PIT workbooks, including alternate filename patterns, raw snapshot metadata, and source-registry lineage.
responsibilities: Resolve PIT workbook URLs by vintage year, including future-year fallbacks and alternate filename patterns.
Download and cache raw PIT files from HUD User with stable metadata sidecars.
Register source hashes and download details so PIT ingests retain auditable lineage.

ai.notes: External-data boundary for HUD PIT inputs. It carries format and URL churn risk because HUD has changed both filenames and workbook extensions across vintages.

summary: Loads recipe YAML, dispatches by schema version, and normalizes read, parse, and validation failures into a single recipe-facing error surface.
responsibilities: Accept recipe input as a file path or pre-parsed mapping for CLI and tests.
Enforce the top-level mapping and version contract before planning begins.
Validate recipe data against the registered schema model and raise stable load errors.

ai.notes: Small but central gateway for all recipe workflows. Most changes affect validation behavior and user-facing error quality more than execution semantics.

summary: Analyzes recipe readiness without executing it by resolving plans, probing schemas and support data, and emitting structured blocking and warning findings.
responsibilities: Resolve per-pipeline execution plans and capture planner failures in a structured report.
Probe datasets, transforms, measures, and geography alignment for the requested year range.
Return machine-readable preflight findings with remediation hints for CLI and automation.

ai.notes: Broad validation layer with many dependencies. It is a likely first failure point when recipe contracts, schemas, or dataset coverage drift.

summary: Maintains the boundary vintage registry, including idempotent upserts, latest-vintage selection, and health checks for missing or temporary files.
responsibilities: Persist the boundary registry parquet with provenance and stable schema handling.
Register, update, list, and delete boundary vintages using content hashes and source labels.
Choose the effective latest boundary vintage and flag broken registry entries during health checks.

ai.notes: Acts as the source of truth for boundary discovery. Policy bugs here can redirect downstream workflows to the wrong boundary vintage even when data exists.

summary: Downloads the Census 2010-2020 tract relationship table, computes bidirectional area-share weights, and writes the canonical parquet with provenance and registry metadata.
responsibilities: Fetch the raw tract relationship file and persist a raw snapshot with a stable content hash.
Validate expected columns and compute 2010-to-2020 and 2020-to-2010 area-share weights.
Save and reload the curated parquet while surfacing actionable missing-file errors.

ai.notes: Foundational cross-era reference ingest. Errors here propagate into tract translation and any panel that spans the 2010 and 2020 tract vintages.

summary: Encodes Connecticut county-to-planning-region transition rules and provides geometry-based crosswalks and remapping helpers for CT-specific datasets.
responsibilities: Define the authoritative Connecticut legacy-county and planning-region FIPS sets and geometry vintages.
Build county and tract crosswalks between legacy counties and planning regions using area overlap.
Remap CT GEOIDs and weighted values across the legacy and planning-region split for downstream pipelines.

ai.notes: Specialized geography utility with outsized impact for Connecticut workflows. Risk is concentrated in edge-case remapping and overlap weighting during the Census county-equivalent transition.

summary: Implements the coclab generate xwalks command, wiring registry lookups, geometry loading, crosswalk builders, diagnostics, and output path policies into one CLI workflow.
responsibilities: Validate CLI options and locate the boundary, tract, and county artifacts needed to build crosswalks.
Invoke tract and county crosswalk builders with optional population weighting and output safeguards.
Surface diagnostics and actionable errors for both human operators and automation.

ai.notes: CLI orchestration layer with moderate fan-out. Most regressions here show up as prerequisite checks, path resolution mistakes, or poor operator guidance rather than incorrect weighting math.

summary: Resolves storage-root configuration from CLI flags, environment variables, repo config, user config, and defaults with predictable precedence and path normalization.
responsibilities: Load repo-local and user YAML config files defensively.
Apply precedence rules across CLI, environment, config files, and built-in defaults.
Normalize relative paths against the correct base directory for each configuration surface.

ai.notes: High fan-in configuration contract. Subtle precedence or path-normalization bugs would redirect reads and writes across many ingest and build workflows.

summary: Aggregates county-level Census PEP populations onto CoC geography using county crosswalk weights, coverage thresholds, and provenance-rich parquet outputs.
responsibilities: Load and reconcile PEP county vintages together with the county crosswalk inputs.
Compute weighted geography-year population aggregates, coverage ratios, and nulling rules.
Write cached CoC-level outputs with provenance and canonical naming.

ai.notes: Combines multiple data vintages and weighting semantics. Regression risk is highest around coverage handling and deduplication when overlapping PEP vintages are present.

summary: Fetches CoC grantee-area boundaries from HUD's ArcGIS feature service, normalizes them to the canonical boundary schema, validates them, and writes GeoParquet plus raw API snapshots.
responsibilities: Fetch paginated GeoJSON features from the HUD ArcGIS service.
Map ArcGIS fields and geometries into the canonical CoC boundary schema.
Persist raw responses, source-registry metadata, and the curated boundary artifact.

ai.notes: Primary current CoC boundary ingest path against a live feature service. Operational risk is mostly upstream API/schema drift and pagination behavior rather than local geometry logic.

summary: Downloads Census PEP county population vintages, reshapes wide CSV extracts into canonical county-year records, and writes provenance-rich curated parquet outputs.
responsibilities: Manage supported postcensal vintages and cached raw downloads.
Normalize Census CSV extracts into long county-year population records.
Register source hashes and write canonical PEP artifacts for downstream aggregation.

ai.notes: External-data boundary with overlapping vintage coverage and series-selection rules. Mistakes here can silently shift county populations across multiple downstream years.

summary: Maintains parquet registries for PIT year files and multi-year PIT vintages, providing idempotent upsert and lookup helpers keyed by year, vintage, and source.
responsibilities: Serialize PIT year and vintage registry entries with parquet-friendly types.
Compute file hashes and idempotently update existing registry rows.
Resolve latest or specific PIT artifact paths for downstream consumers.

ai.notes: This is the source of truth for PIT artifact discovery rather than ingest itself. Registry policy changes can redirect consumers to the wrong vintage even when the underlying files are correct.

summary: Builds area-weighted tract crosswalks for CoC or other target geographies, optionally derives population weights, validates coverage, and writes provenance-tagged parquet outputs.
responsibilities: Overlay target geometries with census tracts in an equal-area CRS to compute area shares.
Derive population-weighted shares and validate per-geography coverage totals.
Persist canonical tract crosswalk artifacts with weighting metadata.

ai.notes: Core geometry-overlay module with both computational cost and methodological impact. CRS, batching, or weighting changes will ripple into every tract-based aggregate that consumes these crosswalks.

summary: Fetches ACS 1-year B23025 unemployment data at CBSA geography, maps CBSAs into Glynn/Fox metro IDs, derives unemployment rates, and writes curated metro parquet outputs.
responsibilities: Issue Census ACS1 API requests and normalize CBSA response columns.
Map CBSA codes to metro definitions and derive unemployment metrics.
Write the metro-level ACS1 artifact with provenance and a stable schema.

ai.notes: Metro-native ingest path that bypasses tract crosswalks, so most fragility comes from Census response shapes and CBSA-to-metro definition drift.

summary: Builds frozen Glynn/Fox claims-audit panel artifacts and source datasets by combining curated PIT, ACS, and ZORI inputs into reproducible metro and CoC audit outputs.
responsibilities: Define the fixed audit workloads, required columns, and selection rules.
Rebuild or reuse source panels and derive modeling-ready audit variables.
Write audit panel artifacts plus provenance and copied reference context for claims-audit workflows.

ai.notes: Special-purpose but user-visible orchestration module for a fixed research workflow. It embeds business rules and hardcoded artifact paths, so drift in upstream panel layout or metro definitions can break it.

summary: Downloads per-state TIGER/Line tract shapefiles, normalizes GEOID and schema differences across vintages, and materializes combined tract geometry artifacts with raw snapshot tracking.
responsibilities: Resolve vintage-specific state ZIP URLs and download tract shapefiles with optional progress reporting.
Combine state shapefiles into a unified tract GeoDataFrame with canonical fields.
Persist raw ZIP snapshots, compute stable hashes, and register source lineage.

ai.notes: Large external-data ingest boundary with many per-state requests. Upstream file availability, vintage-specific path quirks, and GEOID schema variants are the main operational risks.

summary: Ingests HUD CoC boundary data from the current ArcGIS feature service with legacy HUD Exchange fallbacks, normalizes field-name variation across vintages, and produces canonical boundary artifacts.
responsibilities: Page through the ArcGIS feature service with retries and progress reporting.
Download and hash legacy geodatabases or state shapefiles when current endpoints are unavailable.
Normalize heterogeneous HUD field names and emit canonical CoC boundary outputs with lineage metadata.

ai.notes: This is the most defensive HUD boundary ingest path because it bridges multiple historical delivery formats. Complexity is driven by upstream volatility rather than business logic alone.

summary: Computes crosswalk coverage diagnostics and area-versus-population weighting comparisons, then formats the results into operator-facing summaries.
responsibilities: Quantify tract coverage and dominant-tract contributions per target geography.
Compare numeric measures between area-weighted and population-weighted aggregations.
Summarize diagnostic results and highlight problematic geographies for operators.

ai.notes: Not on the main ingest path, but it is the observability layer for crosswalk quality. Regressions here mostly mislead operators rather than corrupt base data directly.

summary: Applies the shared finalization contract for CoC and metro panels, including canonical column order, dtype enforcement, boundary-change flags, provenance aliases, and ZORI-related columns.
responsibilities: Define canonical panel schemas and preferred recipe column aliases.
Classify alignment and detect boundary changes across yearly panel rows.
Coerce output dtypes and column ordering so panel builders emit compatible final artifacts.

ai.notes: Shared output-contract module with high fan-in from panel builders and recipe execution. Column or dtype changes here can cause broad regressions even when upstream calculations are unchanged.

summary: Top-level package init that imports and re-exports the core Phase 2 submodules so consumers can use coclab as a unified namespace.
responsibilities: Re-export census, measures, provenance, and xwalks submodules at the package root.
Define __all__ to document the canonical public surface of coclab.
Signal which submodules constitute Phase 2 of the project.

ai.notes: Thin glue file with no logic. Import errors in any listed submodule will bubble up at package import time, making this an early signal of broken dependencies.

summary: ACS package public surface that re-exports tract ingestion and translation functions from submodules so callers import from a single stable namespace.
responsibilities: Expose fetch_state_tract_data, fetch_tract_data, get_output_path, and ingest_tract_data at the acs package level.
Re-export TranslationStats, needs_translation, get_source_tract_vintage, and both translate helpers.
Define __all__ as the authoritative ACS public API.

ai.notes: Aggregation init with moderate fan-out. Adding or removing symbols here has ripple effects on every caller that imports from coclab.acs directly.

summary: ACS ingest subpackage init that surfaces tract population ingestion functions at the ingest namespace level.
responsibilities: Re-export fetch_state_tract_data, fetch_tract_data, get_output_path, and ingest_tract_data from tract_population.
Define __all__ for the ingest subpackage public surface.

ai.notes: Pure re-export shim. Its main role is allowing callers to import from the ingest package without knowing the internal module layout.

summary: Translates ACS data from 2010 census tract geography to 2020 geography using area-share weights, with proper count and median propagation and margin-of-error handling.
responsibilities: Determine whether ACS vintage data needs tract geography translation based on end-year decennial alignment.
Apply the Census Bureau tract relationship file to proportionally reallocate count columns by area weight.
Propagate median columns via population-weighted averaging and MOE columns via sqrt-of-squared-weights formula.

ai.notes: Methodologically sensitive translation layer. Aggregation mistakes here silently shift demographic counts across the 2010/2020 tract boundary and affect every downstream panel that spans both eras.

summary: Single source of truth for all ACS 5-year Census API variable codes, friendly column names, and classification into count, median, and MOE column groups.
responsibilities: Define ACS_VARIABLES mapping from Census API codes to human-readable column names.
Enumerate adult age-group variables for deriving adult population from sex-by-age tables.
Classify columns into COUNT_COLUMNS, MEDIAN_COLUMNS, and MOE_COLUMNS for use in translation and aggregation.
Specify the canonical TRACT_OUTPUT_COLUMNS ordering for curated parquet files.

ai.notes: High fan-in reference file. Adding, removing, or reclassifying a variable here affects what gets fetched, how it is aggregated, and which columns appear in every downstream ACS artifact.

summary: Defines ACS 1-year variable codes and output schema for CBSA-level unemployment data derived from Census table B23025, separate from 5-year tract variables.
responsibilities: Enumerate ACS 1-year variable codes and friendly names for B23025 employment-status fields.
Define the derived unemployment_rate_acs1 measure and its formula documentation.
Specify canonical column ordering for metro-level ACS 1-year output files.
Record the first reliable ACS 1-year vintage year for validity guards.

ai.notes: Narrowly scoped reference file for ACS 1-year metro products. Low risk in isolation, but changes to variable codes or the output schema would break metro unemployment ingestion and downstream panels.

summary: Defines the canonical analysis geography abstraction separating observation units (CoC, metro) from source geometries, with helpers for geo column normalization across DataFrames.
responsibilities: Model AnalysisGeometryRef as a frozen dataclass encoding geo_type, boundary_vintage, and definition_version.
Provide resolve_geo_col and infer_geo_type helpers for backward-compatible DataFrames that may carry coc_id, metro_id, or geo_id.
Expose ensure_canonical_geo_columns to standardize geo_type and geo_id columns across producers.

ai.notes: Cross-cutting contract module used by build, panel, and aggregation layers. The backward-compatibility rules for coc_id are load-bearing and must stay consistent with the schema transition plan.

summary: Provides named build directory resolution, manifest read/write, aggregate-run recording, and build discovery helpers for the optional build workflow.
responsibilities: Resolve and validate named build directories under a configurable builds root.
Read and write manifest.json files with build metadata, year lists, and aggregate-run histories.
Append structured aggregate-run entries with run IDs, dataset, alignment, output, and status fields.

ai.notes: Utility layer for the builds workflow. The manifest contract is the primary risk surface; callers that read manifest fields directly would break if key names change.

summary: Census package init that aggregates and re-exports TIGER tract, TIGER county, and tract-relationship ingest functions as the census public API.
responsibilities: Re-export download, ingest, and save functions for TIGER tracts and counties.
Re-export the full tract-relationship API including TractRelationshipNotFoundError.
Define __all__ as the census subpackage's stable external interface.

ai.notes: Aggregation init with moderate fan-out from three ingest submodules. Reorganizing ingest submodule names or symbols must be reflected here or callers importing from coclab.census will break silently.

summary: Census ingest subpackage init that surfaces TIGER tract, TIGER county, and tract-relationship ingestion functions at the ingest namespace level.
responsibilities: Re-export download_tiger_tracts, ingest_tiger_tracts, and save_tracts from tiger_tracts.
Re-export download_tiger_counties, ingest_tiger_counties, and save_counties from tiger_counties.
Re-export the tract-relationship API including TractRelationshipNotFoundError.

ai.notes: Pure aggregation init. Adding a new census ingest module requires updating this file or callers importing from coclab.census.ingest will miss the new functions.

summary: Downloads and persists TIGER/Line county shapefiles for a given vintage year, normalizing to EPSG:4326 and saving as GeoParquet. Integrates with the raw-snapshot and source-registry infrastructure to track upstream changes.
responsibilities: Download TIGER/Line ZIP from Census Bureau and persist raw content
Normalize geometry schema to canonical county fields (geoid, geo_vintage, source)
Register downloaded files in the source registry for change detection
Expose a combined ingest_tiger_counties() pipeline function

ai.notes: Has a special-case URL for year 2010. Relies on coclab.paths, coclab.raw_snapshot, and coclab.source_registry — changes to path or registry contracts ripple here.

summary: Package init for coclab.cli, re-exporting the root Typer app.
responsibilities: Re-export the main Typer app object for coclab's CLI entry point

ai.notes: Thin shim; the real CLI logic lives in coclab.cli.main and the individual command modules.

summary: Defines the 'coclab aggregate' Typer command group with sub-commands for aggregating PEP, PIT, ACS, and ZORI source data into CoC-scoped curated artifacts. Supports optional named builds (deprecated) and direct output to data/curated/.
responsibilities: Dispatch PEP, PIT, ACS, and ZORI aggregation sub-commands to domain pipeline functions
Validate alignment modes, year specs, and build directory arguments
Write output files to curated or build-scoped directories and record run metadata
Support deprecated --build flag with backward-compatibility warnings

ai.notes: Large, high-traffic command module; references multiple aggregation pipelines. The deprecated --build path is still present and could confuse callers. Any change to aggregation function signatures requires matching changes here.

summary: CLI function that compares CoC boundaries between two vintages, reporting added, removed, changed, and unchanged CoC geometries via geom_hash comparison. Supports optional CSV export.
responsibilities: Load two boundary vintages from the registry by name
Compute geometry diffs using geom_hash to classify added/removed/changed/unchanged
Display a structured console report of the comparison
Optionally persist the diff to a CSV file

ai.notes: Relies on coclab.registry.registry.list_boundaries(); if registry is empty or geom_hash is absent the command fails gracefully.

summary: CLI function for validating PIT count consistency between two vintage release files, comparing tab-level totals and per-CoC changes across shared years.
responsibilities: Load two PIT vintage parquet files from the registry
Merge on (pit_year, coc_id) key and compute per-record deltas
Display tab-total-by-year comparison and detailed change summary
Exit with code 1 when differences are detected (useful for CI)

ai.notes: Exposes both a private _run_pit_vintages_validation() helper and a public validate_pit_vintages() wrapper; this dual API hints at earlier refactoring.

summary: CLI function that validates population totals produced by the tract-to-CoC crosswalk against ACS national totals, surfacing coverage gaps and area-share anomalies.
responsibilities: Auto-discover or accept explicit crosswalk and ACS tract files
Compute weighted CoC population and compare to ACS national sum
Flag tracts with area_share sums outside [0.99, 1.01] (overlap or partial coverage)
Optionally show state-level disaggregation for diagnosing regional coverage gaps

ai.notes: Internally defines _run_population_validation() and a wrapper validate_population(); the dual structure matches the pit-vintages pattern.

summary: CLI function for running quality diagnostics on a tract-to-CoC crosswalk file, computing per-CoC coverage ratios and tract concentration metrics.
responsibilities: Load a crosswalk parquet and call coclab.measures.diagnostics functions
Report per-CoC coverage and concentration summary to stdout
Identify and display problem CoCs below coverage or above concentration thresholds
Support machine-readable JSON output and optional CSV export

ai.notes: Bridges the CLI surface and the analytics-layer diagnostics module; thin dispatch with error handling.

summary: CLI command that materializes the three metro definition parquet artifacts (definitions, CoC membership, county membership) from in-code Glynn/Fox constants.
responsibilities: Check for pre-existing artifacts unless --force is provided
Call metro.io.write_metro_artifacts() to generate the three canonical parquet files
Report success paths or validation errors in human-readable or JSON format

ai.notes: Short command; correctness depends entirely on coclab.metro.io.write_metro_artifacts. Any version bump to metro definitions requires running this command.

summary: CLI command for ingesting ACS 1-year employment/unemployment data at CBSA geography for Glynn/Fox metros, computing unemployment rates per metro.
responsibilities: Fetch ACS Table B23025 from the Census API for a given vintage year
Map CBSAs to Glynn/Fox metro IDs and compute unemployment rates
Write a curated parquet file and report per-metro summary
Support JSON output mode for programmatic consumers

ai.notes: Requires a Census API key; delegates all fetching/mapping to coclab.acs.ingest.metro_acs1.

summary: CLI command for ingesting ACS 5-year tract-level demographic and economic data from the Census API, with optional translation between tract vintages.
responsibilities: Check for cached output and skip unless --force is specified
Call ingest_tract_data() with acs_vintage and tract_vintage parameters
Validate required output columns and summarize population statistics
Support both human-readable and JSON output formats

ai.notes: Prerequisite for the aggregate acs command; if the cache file is missing, the aggregate pipeline will error. The translation path (2010 tracts -> 2020 tracts) adds complexity.

summary: CLI command for downloading TIGER/Line tract and/or county shapefiles for a given year, with skip-if-exists and --force support.
responsibilities: Validate geometry type argument and determine what to download
Check for existing cached files before downloading
Delegate to census.ingest functions for tracts and counties
Report download paths and handle per-type errors gracefully

ai.notes: Front-end for census.ingest; keeps state simple with a downloaded list for the summary. NHGIS may be preferred for 2010 tracts.

summary: CLI command for downloading census tract and county shapefiles from NHGIS (IPUMS), polling for extract completion and saving as GeoParquet.
responsibilities: Validate API key and year arguments before submitting NHGIS extract requests
Poll extract status with configurable interval until completion or timeout
Normalize downloaded shapefiles to the canonical TIGER schema and persist
Summarize download, skip, and failure outcomes for each year/geo-type combination

ai.notes: Long-running by design (waits up to 60 min); requires IPUMS_API_KEY. Preferred over coclab ingest tiger for 2010 data.

summary: CLI command for ingesting a single-year PIT count file from HUD User: downloads, parses, writes canonical parquet, registers in the PIT registry, and runs QA.
responsibilities: Download the PIT Excel file from HUD User or use an existing raw file
Parse the file into canonical schema using parse_pit_file()
Write curated parquet and register the year in the PIT registry
Run QA validation and report issues without blocking ingestion

ai.notes: Five-step pipeline in one command; QA failures are reported as warnings only. The --parse-only flag skips the download step for local testing.

summary: CLI command for ingesting all historical years from a single PIT vintage release file, capturing the full tab-by-tab historical record.
responsibilities: Download the PIT vintage Excel from HUD User or use an existing raw file
Parse all year tabs using parse_pit_vintage() to capture historical revisions
Write a multi-year vintage parquet and register it in the PIT vintage registry
Run QA validation across all parsed years

ai.notes: Differs from ingest_pit.py in that it captures historical revisions (all tabs) rather than just the current year. The resulting vintage file is the basis for crosscheck_pit_vintages.

summary: CLI command for downloading the Census 2010-to-2020 tract relationship file needed to translate older ACS vintages to current tract geography.
responsibilities: Check for existing relationship file unless --force is given
Delegate download and normalization to coclab.census.ingest.ingest_tract_relationship()
Report output path on success or error details on failure

ai.notes: Prerequisite for ACS ingest when translation between tract vintages is required.

summary: CLI command that scans the TIGER curated directory and displays a formatted table of available census geometry files (tracts and counties) with row counts and metadata.
responsibilities: Parse both new and legacy census filename conventions to extract type and year
Read parquet metadata for row counts without loading full data
Filter by type and report in human-readable or JSON format
Handle missing directory gracefully

ai.notes: Read-only inspection command; low risk. Supports both new (tracts__T{year}) and legacy (tracts__{year}) naming.

summary: CLI command that scans data/curated/ subdirectories and reports every parquet file with its row count, column list, and size.
responsibilities: Enumerate known curated subdirectories (governed by CURATED_SUBDIRS)
Read lightweight parquet metadata for each artifact
Filter to a single subdir if --subdir is specified
Output as a formatted table or JSON array

ai.notes: Depends on CURATED_SUBDIRS constant from curated_policy; new artifact types must be added there before they appear here.

summary: CLI command that lists available CoC measure files, parsing boundary and ACS vintages from filenames and reporting CoC counts, weighting methods, and file metadata.
responsibilities: Scan measures directory for both new and legacy filename patterns
Parse boundary_vintage and acs_vintage from filenames
Read each parquet to extract CoC count and weighting method
Output as a formatted table or JSON list

ai.notes: Loads entire parquet for weighting_method and row count, which may be slow if many large files are present.

summary: CLI command that scans the crosswalks directory and displays available tract and county crosswalk files with type, vintage information, row counts, and file metadata.
responsibilities: Detect both new (xwalk__B{b}xT{t}) and legacy crosswalk filename formats
Filter by crosswalk type (tract / county / all)
Read parquet metadata for row counts without full load
Output as formatted table or JSON

ai.notes: Read-only; supports both canonical and legacy naming. Low operational risk.

summary: CLI command that scans data/curated/ for legacy-named artifacts and proposes or applies canonical renames using the rules in curated_migrate.py.
responsibilities: Call scan_curated_for_migration() to identify rename candidates, duplicates, and unknown files
Display a dry-run log of proposed actions unless --apply is given
Execute renames when --apply is specified
Support JSON output of the migration plan

ai.notes: Destructive when run with --apply; safe by default due to dry-run mode. Should be run after a naming-scheme migration to clean up old files.

summary: CLI command for running the full diagnostics suite on a panel parquet file, reporting coverage ratios, boundary changes, and missingness; supports CSV export.
responsibilities: Load a panel parquet and pass it to coclab.panel.generate_diagnostics_report()
Display a text summary of coverage, boundary changes, and missingness
Export individual diagnostic tables to CSV when --format csv is specified
Support machine-readable JSON output

ai.notes: Thin dispatch to panel.diagnostics; the heavy logic lives there. Useful as a post-build QA step.

summary: CLI command for downloading and normalizing Census Bureau PEP county population estimates, supporting postcensal and auto series selection with year-range filtering.
responsibilities: Validate series, vintage, and year-range arguments
Check for existing output and skip unless --force is used
Call pep.ingest.ingest_pep_county() and report counties, years, and record counts
Read provenance from the output to display the series name used

ai.notes: Aggregation of PEP to CoC geography is a separate command (coclab aggregate pep). Exit codes 2 and 3 distinguish validation vs. download errors.

summary: CLI command for validating and repairing the source registry by checking local file hashes and removing or updating stale entries.
responsibilities: Load registry parquet and iterate over entries with local_path
Check file existence and compare stored vs. current SHA-256 hash
Prompt or apply policy (skip/update/remove/error) for hash mismatches
Save the repaired registry to disk after changes

ai.notes: Potentially destructive (removes registry entries); dry-run mode is the default. The --on-hash-mismatch flag enables non-interactive use in CI.

summary: CLI command for displaying ACS-derived demographic and economic measures for a specific CoC, reading from the appropriate measures parquet file.
responsibilities: Auto-discover or filter measures files by boundary and ACS vintage
Filter the parquet to the requested CoC identifier
Format and display population, income, rent, and coverage statistics
Support table, JSON, and CSV output formats

ai.notes: Read-only display command; suggests similar CoCs when the requested ID is not found. Supports both legacy and canonical filename patterns.

summary: CLI command for a one-shot environment health report that scans all curated asset types, checks for missing prerequisites, and reports named builds.
responsibilities: Scan curated directories for boundaries, census geometries, crosswalks, PIT, ACS, measures, and ZORI
Check for common missing prerequisite combinations and generate actionable issue hints
Scan optional named-build directories and report their manifest status
Output a human-readable or JSON health report; exit 1 when required assets are missing

ai.notes: Useful first command to run in a new environment. The regex-based file discovery must be kept in sync with canonical naming conventions.

summary: CLI command for validating the curated data directory layout, checking for non-canonical filenames, nested subdirectories, and unknown top-level subdirs.
responsibilities: Call curated_policy.validate_curated_layout() with the configured base directory
Group violations by category and display or emit as JSON
Exit with code 1 when any violations are found

ai.notes: Thin wrapper; all logic lives in curated_policy. Run as a CI gate to prevent naming drift.

summary: CLI commands for ZORI ingestion (from Zillow), aggregation from county to CoC geography, and per-CoC ZORI quality diagnostics.
responsibilities: Download and normalize ZORI county data from Zillow Economic Research
Aggregate county ZORI to CoC geography using area-weighted crosswalks and ACS weights
Run per-CoC coverage and dominance diagnostics on a CoC-level ZORI file
Support named-build directories and multiple temporal alignment and weighting options

ai.notes: Three distinct command functions in one module. The aggregate_zori command has a complex prerequisite chain (boundaries, TIGER counties, crosswalks, ZORI ingest).

summary: Utility module for scanning curated artifact directories for legacy-named files and proposing or applying deterministic canonical renames.
responsibilities: Define legacy-to-canonical rename rules using regex patterns and builder functions
Scan all known curated subdirectories for files that do not match canonical patterns
Classify files as rename candidates, duplicates with canonical conflicts, or unknown
Apply renames or produce a dry-run log

ai.notes: Rename rules are defined in _LEGACY_RULES; new migrations require adding entries there. Called by the migrate_curated CLI command.

summary: Defines and enforces the canonical naming and layout policy for the data/curated/ directory tree, including pattern validation per subdirectory.
responsibilities: Declare CURATED_SUBDIRS and CANONICAL_PATTERNS for every artifact type
Validate a curated directory against naming rules and return structured violations
Detect nested subdirectories and unexpected root-level files
Serve as the single source of truth for what constitutes a valid curated layout

ai.notes: High-impact module: adding a new artifact type requires updating CURATED_SUBDIRS and CANONICAL_PATTERNS here, then re-running validate_curated. Changes here affect both the migration and validation commands.

summary: Package init for coclab.geo, re-exporting all public geometry processing symbols from io, normalize, and validate sub-modules.
responsibilities: Re-export GeoParquet I/O helpers (read_geoparquet, write_geoparquet)
Re-export geometry normalization functions (normalize_boundaries, compute_geom_hash, etc.)
Re-export ValidationResult and validate_boundaries

ai.notes: Acts as a stable public API surface for coclab.geo; downstream imports should prefer this module over the sub-modules directly.

summary: GeoParquet I/O utilities for reading and writing boundary data, including provenance injection and resolution of canonical boundary paths across naming schemes.
responsibilities: Read GeoParquet files with error handling for missing files
Write GeoDataFrames to GeoParquet with optional provenance metadata injection
Resolve curated boundary file paths across new, legacy, and very-legacy naming conventions
Provide registry_path() helper for the boundary registry location

ai.notes: resolve_curated_boundary_path() is called widely; any change to boundary filename conventions must be reflected here. Provenance injection via re-open/rewrite pattern adds two file operations.

summary: Geometry normalization pipeline for CoC boundary GeoDataFrames: CRS normalization, geometry repair, polygon-type enforcement, and SHA-256 hash computation.
responsibilities: Normalize all geometries to EPSG:4326
Repair invalid geometries using shapely.make_valid
Ensure all geometries are Polygon or MultiPolygon (extract polygon parts from collections)
Compute stable SHA-256 hashes of normalized WKB representations

ai.notes: The geom_hash column produced here is the primary key for boundary change detection. Precision is pinned at 1e-6 grid_size; changing this breaks hash comparability with existing data.

summary: Boundary schema validation for GeoDataFrames: checks required columns, coc_id uniqueness per vintage, geometry validity, and coordinate anomalies.
responsibilities: Validate presence and types of required columns against the canonical boundary schema
Check coc_id uniqueness within each boundary_vintage group
Flag null, empty, or invalid geometries
Detect anomalous bounding boxes and suspiciously small polygons

ai.notes: Returns a ValidationResult with separate error/warning lists; callers decide how to surface issues. Not called automatically on ingest — must be invoked explicitly.

summary: Package init for coclab.ingest, re-exporting the two boundary ingest entry points from HUD Exchange and HUD OpenData.
responsibilities: Re-export ingest_hud_exchange and ingest_hud_opendata

ai.notes: Thin package init; sources HUD boundary data from two distinct upstream providers.

summary: Package init for coclab.measures, re-exporting ACS variable definitions and the core aggregation and diagnostics functions.
responsibilities: Re-export ACS_VARS and ADULT_VARS variable lists
Re-export aggregate_to_coc and aggregate_to_geo aggregation functions
Re-export crosswalk and measure diagnostic functions

ai.notes: Acts as the public API for ACS measure computation; downstream panels depend on aggregate_to_coc being stable.

summary: Package init for coclab.metro, re-exporting metro definition constants, DataFrame builders, and all metro-level aggregation functions (ACS, PEP, PIT, ZORI).
responsibilities: Re-export metro definition constants (DEFINITION_VERSION, METRO_COUNT, etc.)
Re-export DataFrame builders for definitions, CoC membership, and county membership
Re-export metro-level aggregation functions for all four data sources

ai.notes: High re-export surface; any breaking change in a sub-module aggregation signature must be reflected here and in dependent recipe executors.

summary: Aggregates tract-level ACS data to Glynn/Fox metro areas by building a metro-tract crosswalk from county membership and delegating to the generalized aggregate_to_geo() function.
responsibilities: Build a metro-tract crosswalk with area_share=1.0 from county membership
Aggregate ACS tract data to metro level using the generalized aggregation function
Warn about tracts dropped due to missing county-FIPS match
Attach definition_version to the output

ai.notes: Tracts nest perfectly within counties so area_share is always 1.0; this assumption breaks if non-county-aligned tracts are introduced. Delegates all aggregation math to measures.acs.aggregate_to_geo.

summary: Reads and writes the three curated metro definition parquet artifacts, with validation before writing and convenience functions for loading them.
responsibilities: Build metro definition DataFrames and validate before writing
Write all three tables (definitions, CoC membership, county membership) with provenance
Provide read_metro_* convenience functions for loading each artifact
Expose validate_curated_metro() for post-write integrity checks

ai.notes: Called by the generate_metro CLI command; raises ValueError on validation failure so callers know immediately if the definitions are inconsistent.

summary: Aggregates county-level PEP population estimates to Glynn/Fox metro areas, tracking coverage and listing missing counties per metro-year.
responsibilities: Build a metro-county crosswalk with area_share=1.0 from county membership
Delegate aggregation to pep.aggregate.aggregate_pep_counties()
Compute expected county counts and missing-county lists per metro-year
Attach definition_version to the output

ai.notes: Low risk but depends on county_fips alignment between PEP data and metro membership. Missing counties are tracked in missing_counties column.

summary: Aggregates CoC-level PIT counts to Glynn/Fox metro areas, summing pit_total/sheltered/unsheltered across member CoCs per year with coverage tracking.
responsibilities: Load or accept metro-CoC membership table
Iterate years and metros to sum PIT counts across member CoCs
Track CoC coverage ratio and list missing CoCs
Return metro-year DataFrame with Int64 nullable integer PIT columns

ai.notes: Uses nullable Int64 for PIT counts to preserve null semantics when member CoCs are missing. Year column is normalized (pit_year or year).

summary: Validates the three metro definition DataFrames for structural integrity, identifier formats, referential consistency, and duplicate-free keys.
responsibilities: Check required columns in each of the three metro tables
Validate metro_id (GFnn), coc_id (XX-NNN), and county_fips (5-digit) formats
Verify referential integrity between definitions and membership tables
Detect duplicate rows and definition_version inconsistencies

ai.notes: Called before writing artifacts in metro.io.write_metro_artifacts(); errors raise ValueError. Clean separation of validation logic from I/O.

summary: Aggregates county-level ZORI data to Glynn/Fox metro areas on a monthly basis using ACS-based county weights, and collapses to yearly values.
responsibilities: Build a metro-county crosswalk with area_share=1.0 from county membership
Delegate monthly aggregation to rents.aggregate.aggregate_monthly()
Expose collapse_zori_to_yearly() as a thin wrapper for metro context
Support per-year population-weighted aggregation in aggregate_yearly_zori_to_metro()

ai.notes: Orphan-county detection logs warnings but does not fail. The aggregate_yearly_zori_to_metro variant handles temporal population shifts for multi-year panels.

summary: Package init for coclab.nhgis, re-exporting the ingest_nhgis_tracts entry point.
responsibilities: Re-export ingest_nhgis_tracts from the nhgis.ingest sub-module

ai.notes: Minimal init; ingest_nhgis_counties is intentionally not re-exported here.

summary: Downloads census tract and county shapefiles from NHGIS (IPUMS API), normalizing GISJOIN or GEOID identifiers to a standardized schema and persisting raw and curated outputs.
responsibilities: Submit, poll, and download NHGIS extract requests via ipumspy
Normalize NHGIS shapefile attributes to the canonical TIGER schema (geoid, geo_vintage, source)
Persist raw ZIP snapshots and curated GeoParquet with source registry entries
Support both tracts and counties with year-specific shapefile name mappings

ai.notes: SUPPORTED_YEARS is hardcoded to 2010/2020; adding new decennial years requires extending NHGIS_TRACT_SHAPEFILES and NHGIS_COUNTY_SHAPEFILES. ipumspy API is an external dependency.

summary: Package init for coclab.panel, re-exporting all public symbols for panel assembly, policies, diagnostics, conformance, and ZORI eligibility.
responsibilities: Re-export panel build and save functions
Re-export alignment policies and column constants
Re-export diagnostics, conformance, and ZORI eligibility APIs

ai.notes: High-level re-export surface for the panel assembly system; downstream recipe executors depend on build_panel and save_panel being stable.

summary: Panel diagnostics module providing coverage summary, boundary change detection, missingness analysis, weighting sensitivity comparison, and a consolidated DiagnosticsReport container.
responsibilities: Compute per-year coverage ratio statistics (coverage_summary)
Identify CoCs with boundary vintage changes across years (boundary_change_summary)
Report missing-data patterns per column per year (missingness_report)
Generate a DiagnosticsReport with text/CSV export and a full-panel summary function

ai.notes: Uses resolve_geo_col and infer_geo_type for geometry-agnostic operation across CoC and metro panels. The weighting_sensitivity function requires two separately-built panels.

summary: Defines panel alignment policies as serializable AlignmentPolicy dataclasses, with default functions mapping PIT year to boundary and ACS vintages.
responsibilities: Provide default_boundary_vintage() and default_acs_vintage() mapping functions
Define the AlignmentPolicy dataclass with to_dict/from_dict serialization
Expose DEFAULT_POLICY instance using population weighting and standard 1-year ACS lag
Support custom policies via direct AlignmentPolicy construction

ai.notes: Policy functions are serialized by name for provenance; changes to function names break deserialization of existing manifests. The DEFAULT_POLICY is referenced widely.

summary: Implements ZORI eligibility rules for panel integration: computes exclusion reasons, applies eligibility flags, calculates rent-to-income ratios, and adds provenance columns.
responsibilities: Determine exclusion reasons for each CoC-year based on coverage and ZORI availability
Apply eligibility flag (zori_is_eligible) and null out ineligible ZORI values
Compute rent_to_income as monthly ZORI / monthly income for eligible rows
Provide ZoriProvenance dataclass for tracking rent metric and alignment metadata

ai.notes: High-dominance CoCs are warned about but not excluded — a deliberate design decision. DEFAULT_ZORI_MIN_COVERAGE=0.90 is the policy threshold.

summary: Package init for coclab.pep, re-exporting county-level PEP ingest and aggregation functions.
responsibilities: Re-export ingest_pep_county, aggregate_pep_counties, and aggregate_pep_to_coc

ai.notes: Compact init; the ingest and aggregate modules are the primary PEP workhorses.

summary: Diagnostics module for CoC-level PEP aggregation results, computing per-CoC coverage and flagging geographies with below-threshold population estimates.
responsibilities: Compute per-CoC year counts, coverage ratios, and low-coverage flags
Generate a human-readable text summary of diagnostics
Run diagnostics from a file path and optionally persist the result as parquet
Parameterize the geography identifier column for reuse with metro or other geographies

ai.notes: Analogous to rents.diagnostics; PEP uses DEFAULT_MIN_COVERAGE=0.95 (higher than ZORI's 0.90) reflecting near-complete county coverage expectations.

summary: Package init for coclab.pit, re-exporting PIT registry functions and data types.
responsibilities: Re-export PitRegistryEntry, compute_file_hash, get_pit_path, latest_pit_year, list_pit_years, and register_pit_year

ai.notes: Exposes registry API; the ingest sub-package provides the parsing/download functions which are not re-exported here.

summary: Package init for coclab.pit.ingest, re-exporting all public HUD Exchange download and PIT parser symbols.
responsibilities: Re-export download functions from hud_exchange (download_pit_data, get_pit_source_url, etc.)
Re-export parse functions from parser (parse_pit_file, parse_pit_vintage, write_pit_parquet, etc.)
Expose CANONICAL_COLUMNS and error types (PITParseError, InvalidCoCIdError)

ai.notes: Central import surface for PIT ingest; the CLI ingest_pit and ingest_pit_vintage commands import exclusively from here.

summary: Quality assurance module for PIT data: checks for duplicates, missing CoCs relative to boundaries, invalid counts, and extreme year-over-year changes.
responsibilities: Check for duplicate coc_id within the same pit_year
Validate numeric and non-negative counts in pit_total, pit_sheltered, pit_unsheltered
Optionally compare against boundary CoC universe to detect missing CoCs
Flag extreme (>50%) year-over-year changes as warnings

ai.notes: All checks flag rather than auto-correct. validate_pit_data() is the main entry point; boundary comparison is optional and skipped if no vintage is provided.

summary: Package init for coclab.recipe, importing RecipeV1, vintage spec types, and the recipe loader.
responsibilities: Re-export RecipeV1, VintageSetRule, VintageSetSpec, and expand_year_spec from recipe_schema
Re-export load_recipe from loader

ai.notes: Minimal init; the executor, planner, and adapter sub-modules are not re-exported here, keeping the public API surface small.

summary: In-memory DataFrame and file-identity cache for recipe execution, avoiding redundant parquet reads and computing SHA-256 hashes for provenance tracking.
responsibilities: Cache DataFrames keyed by resolved file path for the duration of a recipe run
Return copies to prevent mutation of cached state
Compute and cache SHA-256 + file size (FileIdentity) for provenance embedding
Expose cached_count property for diagnostics

ai.notes: Cache is scoped to a single recipe execution and not persisted. The FileIdentity.path field stores the original string path, not a normalized relative path.

summary: Bootstrap module that registers all built-in geometry and dataset adapters into their respective registries in one idempotent call.
responsibilities: Call register_geometry_defaults() to register CoC, tract, county, and metro adapters
Call register_dataset_defaults() to register HUD PIT, Census ACS/PEP, and Zillow ZORI adapters
Provide a single register_defaults() entry point safe to call multiple times

ai.notes: Must be called before recipe validation or execution; typically called at executor startup. Adding a new geometry or dataset type requires updating the corresponding default_*_adapters module.

summary: Defines and registers built-in dataset validation adapters for HUD PIT, Census ACS5/ACS1/PEP, and Zillow ZORI dataset specifications.
responsibilities: Validate version, native geometry type, and params for each built-in dataset kind
Produce ValidationDiagnostic objects for errors and warnings
Register all adapters into a DatasetAdapterRegistry via register_dataset_defaults()

ai.notes: Known params lists (known_params sets) must be kept in sync with recipe schema evolution. New dataset types require adding a validator and registration call here.

summary: Defines and registers built-in geometry validation adapters for CoC, tract, county, and metro geometry types used in recipe specifications.
responsibilities: Validate vintage plausibility and format constraints per geometry type
Enforce that metro geometry must have a source (definition version) set
Register adapters into a GeometryAdapterRegistry via register_geometry_defaults()

ai.notes: Metro adapter enforces source as required (error) and warns on unexpected vintage. Tract and county adapters warn when vintage is not a decennial year.

summary: Recipe provenance manifest system: defines AssetRecord and RecipeManifest dataclasses, serialization/deserialization, and a bundle export function for replication.
responsibilities: Define AssetRecord and RecipeManifest data models with to/from dict/JSON serialization
Write and read manifests as JSON files on disk
Resolve asset paths across logical storage roots (asset_store, output, legacy project-relative)
Export a self-contained replication bundle by copying all consumed assets

ai.notes: Security: export_bundle validates that asset paths stay within project_root (path traversal prevention). Root-aware (root field) and legacy (root=None) manifests must both be handled.

summary: Non-executing dataset and transform probe functions for recipe preflight validation, checking schemas, column presence, temporal filter applicability, and static broadcast safety.
responsibilities: Probe year and geo-ID column detectability from a parquet schema
Check that required measure columns are present in a dataset
Detect unsafe implicit static-data broadcast across multi-year universes
Validate transform artifact existence on disk and resolve generation readiness

ai.notes: Probes use pyarrow.parquet for schema-only reads (no DataFrame load). The probe_static_broadcast logic is a key safety guard against silent data quality bugs in multi-year recipes.

summary: Package init for coclab.registry, re-exporting boundary registry functions and the RegistryEntry schema type.
responsibilities: Re-export RegistryEntry, RegistryHealthReport, RegistryHealthIssue
Re-export check_registry_health, latest_vintage, list_boundaries, and register_vintage

ai.notes: The public API for the boundary version registry; downstream CLI and ingest code imports from here.

summary: Defines the RegistryEntry dataclass and REGISTRY_COLUMNS list for the boundary vintage registry, supporting parquet serialization/deserialization.
responsibilities: Define RegistryEntry with boundary_vintage, source, ingested_at, path, feature_count, hash_of_file fields
Provide to_dict() and from_dict() for JSON/parquet round-trips
Handle pandas Timestamp and string formats for ingested_at during deserialization
Define REGISTRY_COLUMNS for consistent parquet schema

ai.notes: Core schema contract for the boundary registry; any field addition here requires a migration if existing registry parquet files are present.

summary: Package init for coclab.rents, re-exporting all public ZORI ingestion, weighting, aggregation, and diagnostics symbols.
responsibilities: Re-export ingest_zori from rents.ingest
Re-export county weight functions from rents.weights
Re-export ZORI aggregation and path helpers from rents.aggregate
Re-export diagnostics functions (summarize_coc_zori, identify_problem_cocs, etc.)

ai.notes: Large re-export surface; the rents package is a central domain module touching ingestion, weights, aggregation, and diagnostics. Stability of aggregate_zori_to_coc is critical.

summary: Per-CoC ZORI diagnostics module: computes coverage and dominance statistics, generates a console-friendly text summary, and identifies problem CoCs.
responsibilities: Compute per-CoC coverage and dominance statistics across all months or years (compute_coc_diagnostics)
Generate a multi-section text summary with flagged CoC lists (generate_text_summary)
Provide a unified entry point for CLI diagnostics (summarize_coc_zori and run_zori_diagnostics)
Identify and return problem CoCs with human-readable issue strings (identify_problem_cocs)

ai.notes: Supports both monthly (date column) and yearly (year column) CoC ZORI files. DEFAULT_MIN_COVERAGE=0.90 and DEFAULT_DOMINANCE_THRESHOLD=0.80 are the key policy constants.

summary: Package init for coclab.viz, re-exporting the render_coc_map entry point.
responsibilities: Re-export render_coc_map from viz.map_folium

ai.notes: Minimal init for the visualization package; only one public function currently.

summary: Renders interactive Folium HTML maps for individual CoC boundaries, resolving the boundary file from the registry and fitting the map to the geometry's bounding box.
responsibilities: Resolve vintage and load the corresponding boundary GeoParquet
Filter to the requested CoC and build a Folium map with tooltip metadata
Save the map as an HTML file in data/curated/maps/ or a specified path
Normalize CoC ID for case/whitespace-insensitive matching

ai.notes: Depends on folium and geopandas being installed; the maps are saved as HTML and not version-tracked. Only one CoC is rendered per call.

summary: Package init for coclab.xwalks, re-exporting both tract and county crosswalk builders and helpers.
responsibilities: Re-export build_tract_crosswalk and build_coc_tract_crosswalk from xwalks.tract
Re-export build_county_crosswalk and build_coc_county_crosswalk from xwalks.county
Re-export save_crosswalk, add_population_weights, and validate_population_shares

ai.notes: Crosswalk functions are depended on by the aggregate pipeline and recipe executor; stability of the output schema (area_share, boundary_vintage, etc.) is critical.

summary: Builds area-weighted county-to-analysis-geography crosswalks using geopandas overlay, with a backward-compatible CoC-specific wrapper.
responsibilities: Reproject geometries to equal-area CRS before computing intersection areas
Compute area_share as intersection_area / county_area for county-to-CoC aggregation
Return a crosswalk DataFrame with geometry area columns for provenance and diagnostics
Save crosswalk parquet with provenance via save_county_crosswalk()

ai.notes: geo_id_col parameter makes this geometry-neutral (works for CoC, metro, etc.). The Albers equal-area CRS (ESRI:102003) is hardcoded; changing it would affect all existing area_share values.

summary: Parses compact year-specification strings (single years, ranges, or comma-separated mixtures) into sorted, deduplicated integer lists for build configuration.
responsibilities: Tokenize a year-spec string by commas
Expand range tokens (e.g., '2018-2024') into year lists
Validate year bounds (2000-2099) and reject backwards ranges
Return a sorted, deduplicated list of integer years

ai.notes: Used widely in CLI commands via the --years flag. Expanding MIN_YEAR/MAX_YEAR requires changing constants; the bounds are intentionally conservative.

summary: BLS LAUS series ID contract for Glynn/Fox metro areas. Correct 20-char format: LAUMT + state_fips(2) + cbsa(5) + 000000(6) + measure(2). Example: New York (state_fips=36, cbsa=35620) → LAUMT363562000000003. Measure codes: 03=rate, 04=unemployed, 05=employed, 06=labor_force. Codes 07/08 are NOT available for metro-area series. build_laus_series_id(cbsa, measure, state_fips) is the entry point; requires state_fips because BLS area segment encodes both state and CBSA.

BLS LAUS annual-average metro ingest. Uses correct series IDs (LAUMT + state_fips(2) + cbsa(5) + 000000 + measure(2)) via METRO_STATE_FIPS from definitions. Fetches 25 metros × 4 measures (100 series) in two batches of 50, extracts period M13 (annual average), writes curated Parquet to data/curated/laus/ via naming.laus_metro_path. Fails fast with ValueError if all measure values are null (prevents silent all-null parquet writes). Key functions: ingest_laus_metro, fetch_laus_annual_averages, _build_metro_series_map.

CLI command 'coclab ingest laus-metro' for BLS LAUS annual-average metro ingest. Supports --year (single year), --start-year/--end-year (backfill range), --json for machine-readable output, --api-key for BLS registration key, --definition-version. Loops over years for backfill; emits structured JSON or human-readable summary. Delegates to coclab.ingest.bls_laus.ingest_laus_metro.

Test suite for BLS LAUS metro ingest covering: (1) 20-char series ID format contract (LAUMT + state_fips(2) + cbsa(5) + 000000 + measure(2)) for all 25 Glynn/Fox metros; (2) mocked BLS API fetch and annual-average extraction; (3) full ingest integration (25-metro parquet output, typed columns, naming); (4) naming conventions; (5) conformance column regression guard (unemployment_rate must remain in ACS_MEASURE_COLUMNS); (6) LAUS panel loader integration (_load_laus_metro_measures); (7) CLI tests for coclab ingest laus-metro (--year, --start-year/--end-year, --json, error cases); (8) recipe integration: LausPolicy schema round-trips, _validate_bls_laus adapter, example recipe metro25-glynnfox-laus.yaml loads and passes adapter validation.

Transform artifact resolution helpers extracted from coclab.recipe.executor: maps a recipe transform spec to its on-disk crosswalk path, identifies metro vs CoC vs base geometry roles, and materializes generated metro transform parquets on demand. Re-exported from coclab.recipe.executor to preserve the lazy probe import contract.

Manifest, provenance, and output-path helpers extracted from coclab.recipe.executor: builds the embedded parquet provenance dict and the sidecar RecipeManifest, plus canonical pipeline target/panel-output path resolution. resolve_pipeline_artifacts is the public CLI entry point. Re-exported from coclab.recipe.executor for backwards compatibility.

Connecticut planning-region to legacy-county alignment helpers extracted from coclab.recipe.executor. Detects when a recipe crosswalk uses legacy CT counties while a dataset uses planning regions, builds an authoritative bridge crosswalk via build_ct_county_planning_region_crosswalk, and translates measure values across the boundary. Cache key shape is centralized in _ct_county_alignment_cache_key.

Dataset input preparation helpers extracted from coclab.recipe.executor: column auto-detection (year/geo), year filtering, measure-column validation, implicit static-broadcast rejection, temporal-filter application (point_in_time/calendar_mean/calendar_median/interpolate_to_month), and support-dataset loading for weighted transforms. Each helper turns a raw dataset DataFrame into the year-sliced, column-validated form the resample step expects.

Resample algorithms extracted from coclab.recipe.executor: identity (passthrough), aggregate (many-to-few via crosswalk), and allocate (few-to-many via crosswalk), plus _attach_dynamic_pop_share which derives population weights from a transforms declared support dataset. Owns the _XWALK_JOIN_KEYS / _XWALK_NON_GEO_COLS constants and the _detect_xwalk_target_col helper. _execute_resample in coclab.recipe.executor remains the orchestration glue.

Shared primitives for the recipe executor decomposition: ExecutorError, ExecutionContext, StepResult, PipelineResult, and the _classify_path/_echo/_record_step_note/_get_transform helpers. Every extracted executor submodule and executor.py itself imports from this module, which exists solely to break the partial-initialization import cycle (coclab-l6be) between executor.py and its decomposed siblings.

Panel assembly from recipe execution intermediates: year-frame gathering, target metadata stamping, ZORI/ACS1/LAUS policy application, finalize_panel shaping, and cohort selector. Pure transformation; no I/O. Paired with executor_persistence in the coclab-anb0 panel/persistence split.

Parquet/manifest/diagnostics persistence for recipe execution. Consumes an AssembledPanel from executor_panel and writes the panel parquet (with embedded provenance), *.manifest.json, and *__diagnostics.json sidecar. Reads panel_policy only via executor_panel_policies' conformance-flag helper. Part of the coclab-anb0 split.

Panel policy appliers (ZORI, ACS1, LAUS) as strategy objects with a shared PanelPolicyApplier protocol. DEFAULT_APPLIERS captures ordering invariant. Exposes collect_conformance_flags so assembly and persistence share a single policy-read path. Part of the coclab-anb0 split.
